title: 'Cocktail 2' BO collection: Shahid's film eyes Rs 85 cr despite 'Welcome to the Jungle' clash
description: The Homi Adajania directorial 'Cocktail 2' is heading into its tenth day of release. The Shahid Kapoor, Rashmika Mandanna and Kriti Sanon starrer faces a direct box office battle with the newly released multi-starrer 'Welcome to the Jungle.' While Akshay Kumar's adventure-comedy captured a vast majority of single-screen layouts and prime-time multiplex slots over the weekend, 'Cocktail 2' leveraged glowing word-of-mouth to post a strong 6.3% second Saturday trajectory. Backed by solid international collection and steady urban multi-plex footfalls, the film is steadily veering towards the coveted Rs. 100 crore domestic club.

This development is vital for India's growing ambitions in space exploration and commercial launches.

Party leader Amit Malviya accused opposition leaders of misrepresenting defence minister Rajnath Singh's statement and demanded an apology for undermining the sacrifices of Indian soldiers. The BJP asserted Operation Sindoor was a major success, neutralizing over 100 terrorists and damaging Pakistani military infrastructure, while emphasizing the government's consistent honor for fallen heroes.

Four individuals faced hefty penalties totaling Rs 2 lakh and had their vehicles impounded for violating the Wildlife Protection Act. One offender was caught chasing a Tibetan gazelle within a sanctuary. This strict enforcement aims to safeguard Ladakh's fragile ecosystem and endangered wildlife, with authorities warning of continued stringent action against violators.

title: US government seizes hundreds of websites for illegally streaming FIFA World Cup matches
description: US authorities have dismantled nearly 400 websites illegally streaming FIFA World Cup matches, a move aimed at disrupting criminal networks profiting from piracy.  This operation, dubbed 'Operation Offsides,' highlights the US commitment to protecting intellectual property.  Viewers are warned of significant risks, including malware and data compromise, when accessing unauthorized streams.  International collaboration was key to seizing domains in multiple countries.

title: Home loan mistakes that could cost you more, and how to avoid them
description: Securing a home loan is one of the critical steps in homeownership, yet many buyers stumble over common pitfalls. Avoiding these mistakes, such as not checking eligibility, ignoring credit scores, or borrowing beyond your means, can prevent financial stress and loan rejection. Thoroughly comparing lenders, understanding loan terms, and saving for a down payment are crucial for a smoother home-buying journey.

title: Garg Aviation cadet pilot hurt while deboarding training plane with propeller running
description: A trainee pilot suffered severe back injuries after being hit by a running propeller while deboarding a trainer aircraft at Kanpur's Chakeri Airport. The Directorate General of Civil Aviation (DGCA) has launched a probe into the incident, highlighting a concerning practice of disembarking with engines running to expedite training. This raises questions about safety standards in some Indian flying training organizations, despite the booming aviation sector.

title: Why India's AMCA still needs a US engine, and so do many of the world's fighters
description: India's ambitious fifth-generation fighter jet program faces a significant hurdle as the cost of crucial US-made engines has reportedly tripled, jeopardizing timelines and budgets. This reliance on foreign propulsion systems, also impacting the Tejas Mk2, highlights a persistent challenge in developing world-class fighter jet engines domestically. While other nations also depend on foreign engines initially, India's pursuit of strategic autonomy hinges on mastering this complex technology.

title: Inside Tiruvallur’s ammonia gas leak nightmare
description: The ammonia gas leak at a seafood processing unit in Tiruvallur district, near Chennai, has emerged as one of Tamil Nadu’s deadliest industrial disasters in recent years. This report traces what exactly happened, through eyewitness accounts, survivor testimonies, and medical evidence. The incident also raises serious questions about industrial safety, emergency preparedness, and the conditions of migrants employed far away from home

The two athletes have been exchanging messages, supporting each other's careers. Djokovic, aiming for his 25th Grand Slam, hopes to meet Kohli in India soon, a sentiment echoed by Kohli's ambition for a World Cup win in 2027. Their shared dedication to excellence transcends their respective sports.

description: A Jammu and Kashmir paediatrician, Muzafar Ahmed, has been identified by the NIA as a key conspirator in the deadly 2025 Red Fort bomb blast. Ahmed, a founding member of an alleged Al-Qaeda offshoot, is accused of playing a central role in the terror module's operations, including the manufacture and testing of explosives. The NIA's supplementary chargesheet also names two other individuals involved in supporting the module.

Ninety percent of these sites are at high or critical risk from hazards like floods and wildfires.  However, with many projects still in development, incorporating resilience measures now, costing around 2% of CAPEX, could slash severe loss exposure by 75%, ensuring sustainable energy infrastructure.

Driven by social and family pressures to prevent an arranged marriage, the couple meticulously planned the crime, even attempting it earlier. Police recreated the scene and seized evidence, with a fast-track court now appointed for swift justice.

The Trust assures devotees that all offerings are secure and a fair investigation is underway.

description: India's dependency on imported crude oil has exceeded 90%, creating a significant risk according to EY's findings. The consultancy emphasizes the urgent need for enhancing strategic oil reserves, which presently last just five days compared to China's extensive reserves. This is essential as domestic production wanes and fuel consumption rises, making India vulnerable to global supply disruptions and geopolitical tensions. A thorough reserve strategy is strongly advised.

Dressed in a full suit and tie, Mamdani's dive into the Thomas Jefferson Pool in East Harlem also celebrated 90 years of public swimming in the city, a legacy initiated by Mayor Fiorello LaGuardia.

title: Villagers carry pregnant woman on cot across river in MP, she delivers on way
description: A pregnant woman in Madhya Pradesh's Chhindwara district was carried on a cot across a river due to the absence of a bridge, delaying emergency medical care. Savita Vishwakarma delivered her baby mid-journey after an ambulance couldn't reach her remote village. This incident highlights the critical need for better infrastructure in the area, where residents have long demanded a bridge to ensure timely access to healthcare.

She expressed deep pain and urged for swift justice, criticizing the bail granted to her daughter-in-law, which she believes sends a dangerous message.

title: Agniveer Result 2026: Final merit list date and time expected soon; here is what we know so far
description: The Indian Army is expected to release the Agniveer 2026 final merit list in the first week of July for candidates who appeared in the Phase 2 Common Entrance Examination held from June 1 to 12. The ARO-wise selection lists will be available on the official portal. Shortlisted candidates must complete document verification and medical checks before receiving joining instructions. Training for selected recruits is expected to begin later in July 2026.

'Meant to relieve pain': Rat poison capsules given at Moharram procession in Mumbai
description: A Pune man was apprehended in Byculla for distributing capsules containing zinc phosphide, a potent rat poison, to devotees during a Moharram procession. One devotee fell ill after consuming a capsule and required hospitalization. The accused claimed the capsules were for pain relief during mourning rituals. Police are investigating his motives, mental state, and how he obtained the large quantity of toxic substances.

Driven by social pressures and a desire to prevent an arranged marriage, the duo executed their plan, making the fatal push appear accidental.

'Rape is legal term, not medical': HC says torn hymen no ground to doubt survivor’s testimony
description: The Allahabad High Court upheld a 1983 rape conviction, emphasizing that a survivor's reliable testimony trumps medical findings about a hymen's condition. Justice Santosh Rai stated rape is a legal, not medical, term, and a torn hymen doesn't automatically grant an accused benefit of doubt. The court also corrected the original sentence, imposing a fine and directing the convicted man to surrender.

Jeff Bezos vs Zohran Mamdani debate: Kevin O’Leary says Amazon founder should run cities
description: Amazon founder Jeff Bezos sparked a debate on government efficiency and taxation, suggesting New York City's services would falter under his company's operational model. Billionaire Kevin O'Leary echoed these sentiments, calling the city a 'disaster' and warning against taxing the wealthy. Former Mayor Bill de Blasio criticized Bezos' remarks as out of touch. Meanwhile, Bezos also dismissed fears of AI causing job losses, predicting a labor shortage instead.

title: Michael Dell updates on Dell's Texas move days after Abbott's 'welcome home' message
description: Dell Technologies is officially moving its legal base from Delaware to Texas, with 97% of shareholders approving the change. CEO Michael Dell expressed pride in returning the company's legal home to the state where it was founded. This move aligns with Texas's growing appeal for major corporations, including Tesla, Coinbase, and Exxon Mobil, due to its favorable business climate and talent pool.

title: '1.66 cr records scraped': Class 12 student Sarthak Sidhant, who flagged CBSE tender discrepancies, launches public portal on govt procurement
description: A Class 12 student, Sarthak Sidhant, has launched a public government procurement portal, making over 1.66 crore records accessible. This initiative follows his viral analysis of CBSE's digital evaluation tender, which sparked national debate. Sidhant's work, highlighting alleged discrepancies in tender documents, led to him presenting findings to a Parliamentary committee. He now invites collaboration to build data visualization tools for citizens
